# OPC UA PubSub Subscriber for PHP — Full Documentation

> Pure PHP OPC UA PubSub Subscriber (Part 14). Extension of `php-opcua/opcua-client`. Listens to UADP or JSON NetworkMessages over UDP unicast and multicast. Sign and SignAndEncrypt group-key security. All types use public readonly properties.

Package: php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-transport-pubsub
Repository: https://github.com/php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-transport-pubsub
Packagist: https://packagist.org/packages/php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-transport-pubsub
License: MIT
PHP: >= 8.2
Dependencies: ext-sockets, php-opcua/opcua-client ^4.4, psr/log ^3.0, psr/event-dispatcher ^1.0 (PSR packages are interface-only, zero runtime code)

---

## 1. Introduction

`php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-transport-pubsub` is the Subscriber role of OPC UA PubSub (Part 14) written in pure PHP. It consumes `NetworkMessage` frames — UADP binary or JSON — over UDP unicast and multicast, demuxes them on `(publisherId, writerGroupId, dataSetWriterId)`, and hands decoded `DataSetMessage` payloads to the application as typed DTOs with named fields.

The package is strictly additive to the core `php-opcua/opcua-client`: installing it does not change any core name, any core behaviour, or any core dependency. All symbols live under `PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\*`.

### Installation

```
composer require php-opcua/opcua-client-ext-transport-pubsub
```

### Features

- UDP transport — unicast and IPv4 multicast with `MCAST_JOIN_GROUP` (legacy `IP_ADD_MEMBERSHIP` fallback), `SO_REUSEADDR`, non-blocking sockets, `socket_select` timeout, configurable receive buffer and TTL
- UADP codec — Part 14 §6.2 binary PubSub encoding. PublisherId Byte/UInt16/UInt32/UInt64/String. GroupHeader with WriterGroupId, GroupVersion, NetworkMessageNumber, SequenceNumber. PayloadHeader with one or more DataSetWriterIds. Optional network-message timestamp. Variant, RawData, and DataValue field encodings
- JSON codec — reversible encoding from Part 14 §7.2. Every field carries `{"Type": int, "Body": value}` so round-trip does not need external metadata
- Subscriber runtime — event loop over N transports, demux by `(publisherId, writerGroupId, dataSetWriterId)`, blocking `run()` and non-blocking `poll(int $timeoutMs)`, `stop()` safe from a signal handler
- PubSub security — Sign (HMAC-SHA256) and SignAndEncrypt (AES-256-CBC). `StaticGroupKeyProvider` for pre-shared keys or `SksGroupKeyProvider` for live rotation via a classic `OpcUaClientInterface` (`GetSecurityKeys`, Part 14 §8.4.2). Supported policies: `PubSub-Aes256-CTR` (32+32+4) and `PubSub-Aes128-CTR` (32+16+4). Rejected payloads surface as `SecurityValidationFailed` events before reaching user callbacks
- Metadata loaders — `DataSetMetaData::fromArray`, `fromJsonFile`, `fromXmlString`, `fromXmlFile`, `fromBinary`, `fetchFromServer`. XML accepts both the compact `<DataSetMetaData>` schema and the OPC UA `<DataSetMetaDataType>` / `<FieldMetaData>` naming. `fromBinary` walks the Part 14 §6.2.2 encoding. `fetchFromServer` reads a Variable via a connected classic client and honours already-decoded codec results
- PSR-3 logging — any PSR-3 logger (Monolog, Laravel, etc.); `NullLogger` by default
- PSR-14 events — 8 event classes covering lifecycle, transport, decode, and security. `NullPubSubEventDispatcher` by default (zero overhead)
- Pluggable transports — `PubSubTransportInterface` is the stable extension point. `UdpTransport` is the built-in implementation. Third-party packages (e.g. MQTT) plug in without touching this package
- Module system — `PubSubModule` base class with a `boot(PubSubKernelInterface)`, `onDataSetMessage(...)`, `reset()` lifecycle. `DataSetReaderModule` is the built-in module carrying reader configs
- Testing utilities — `FakeTransport` and `CollectingDispatcher` under `tests/Unit/Kernel/` for driving the kernel without real sockets. The core's `MockClient` is used for tests that exercise `fetchFromServer` and `SksGroupKeyProvider`
- Typed everywhere — every DTO uses `public readonly` properties; no arrays, no magic accessors

### Property access style

All type classes and result DTOs use public readonly properties:

- `$msg->fields` instead of `$msg->getFields()`
- `$field->name` and `$field->value` instead of `$field['name']`
- `$opts->interface` and `$opts->ttl` instead of `$opts->getInterface()`

### Architecture

```
SubscriberBuilder (entry point, fluent config)
  +-- listenUdp(endpoint, UdpOptions, readers, PubSubSecurityOptions?)
  +-- listenOn(transports[], readers[], PubSubSecurityOptions?)

Subscriber (proxy, implements OpcUaSubscriberInterface)
  +-- run() / poll(int $timeoutMs): DataSetMessage[] / stop() / isRunning()

Kernel/PubSubKernel (event-loop infrastructure)
  +-- Transport[]           PubSubTransportInterface instances
  +-- NetworkMessageCodec    UadpNetworkMessageCodec or JsonNetworkMessageCodec
  +-- PubSubSecurityOptions? unwrap() before decode
  +-- PubSubModule[]          DataSetReaderModule + any custom modules
  +-- EventDispatcherInterface PSR-14 dispatch
  +-- LoggerInterface          PSR-3 structured logging

Module/PubSubModule (abstract)
Module/DataSetReaderModule (built-in)

Transport/
  PubSubTransportInterface   open / close / poll / isOpen / transportUri
  UdpTransport               ext-sockets implementation
  UdpOptions                 readonly DTO
  ReceivedPayload            readonly DTO (data, sourceUri, receivedAt, metadata)

Encoding/
  NetworkMessageCodec          interface
  UadpNetworkMessageCodec      encode/decode
  UadpDataSetMessageCodec      encode/decode
  JsonNetworkMessageCodec      encode/decode
  JsonDataSetMessageCodec      encode/decode

Security/
  PubSubSecurityMode           enum (None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt)
  PubSubSecurityOptions        unwrap(string $payload): string
  GroupKeyProviderInterface    signingKey/encryptingKey/keyNonce/refresh
  StaticGroupKeyProvider       pre-shared keys
  SksGroupKeyProvider          calls GetSecurityKeys via classic client

Types/
  NetworkMessage, DataSetMessage, DataSetField, FieldMetaData, DataSetMetaData,
  PublishedDataSet, DataSetReaderConfig, ReaderGroupConfig, PubSubConnectionConfig,
  FieldEncoding

Event/ (PSR-14)
  NetworkMessageReceived, DataSetMessageReceived, DataSetFieldReceived,
  MessageDecodeError, SecurityValidationFailed,
  TransportOpened, TransportClosed, TransportError

Exception/
  UnsupportedTransportException, PubSubDecodeException,
  InvalidDataSetReaderException, PubSubSecurityException
```

---

## 2. Quick Start

### Simplest possible subscriber

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\SubscriberBuilder;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Types\DataSetMetaData;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Types\DataSetReaderConfig;

$metadata = DataSetMetaData::fromJsonFile('/etc/opcua/line1.json');

$subscriber = SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->onDataSetMessage(function ($msg) {
        foreach ($msg->fields as $field) {
            echo "{$field->name} = {$field->getScalar()}\n";
        }
    })
    ->listenUdp(
        endpoint: 'opc.udp://239.0.0.1:4840',
        readers: [new DataSetReaderConfig(
            publisherId: 100,
            writerGroupId: 1,
            dataSetWriterId: 1,
            dataSetMetaData: $metadata,
        )],
    );

$subscriber->run();
```

### Non-blocking polling

```php
while (! $shouldStop) {
    foreach ($subscriber->poll(timeoutMs: 250) as $msg) {
        handle($msg);
    }
    doOtherWork();
}
```

### Graceful shutdown from a signal handler

```php
pcntl_async_signals(true);
pcntl_signal(SIGTERM, fn () => $subscriber->stop());
pcntl_signal(SIGINT, fn () => $subscriber->stop());

$subscriber->run();
```

---

## 3. SubscriberBuilder

Entry point. Fluent configuration chain ending in one of the `listen*()` terminals.

```php
SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->setLogger(LoggerInterface $logger)
    ->setEventDispatcher(EventDispatcherInterface $dispatcher)
    ->onDataSetMessage(callable $callback)
    ->addModule(PubSubModule $module)
    ->setCodec(NetworkMessageCodec $codec)
    ->useJson()
    ->listenUdp(string $endpoint, UdpOptions $transport, array $readers, ?PubSubSecurityOptions $security): Subscriber;

SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->listenOn(array $transports, array $readers, ?PubSubSecurityOptions $security): Subscriber;
```

`onDataSetMessage` registers a callback signature `function (DataSetMessage $msg, int|string $publisherId, int $writerGroupId, string $transportUri): void`. Multiple callbacks can be registered; they are invoked in registration order.

`useJson()` is a shortcut for `setCodec(new JsonNetworkMessageCodec())`.

`listenOn()` accepts any array of `PubSubTransportInterface` instances — use it for tests (`FakeTransport`) or for third-party transports (`MqttTransport` from `opcua-client-ext-mqtt`, when that package exists).

---

## 4. Transports

### UDP

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Transport\UdpOptions;

new UdpOptions(
    interface: '0.0.0.0',        // 0.0.0.0 binds all IPv4, :: binds all IPv6
    receiveBufferSize: 65536,    // SO_RCVBUF
    ttl: 32,                     // multicast TTL / hop limit
    reuseAddress: true,          // SO_REUSEADDR (+ SO_REUSEPORT when available)
);
```

Endpoint format is `opc.udp://HOST:PORT`. Host may be a unicast IP, an IPv4 multicast address (224.0.0.0–239.255.255.255, automatically joined via `MCAST_JOIN_GROUP` with a legacy `IP_ADD_MEMBERSHIP` fallback), or a hostname that resolves to one of the above.

Sockets are made non-blocking after bind; `poll(int $timeoutMs)` uses `socket_select` with the requested timeout split into seconds and microseconds.

### Custom transport

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Transport\PubSubTransportInterface;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Transport\ReceivedPayload;

class MyTransport implements PubSubTransportInterface
{
    public function open(): void { /* ... */ }
    public function close(): void { /* ... */ }
    public function poll(int $timeoutMs): ?ReceivedPayload { /* ... */ }
    public function isOpen(): bool { /* ... */ }
    public function transportUri(): string { /* ... */ }
}

$subscriber = SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->listenOn(transports: [new MyTransport()], readers: [$reader]);
```

The contract is synchronous on purpose. Fiber-based transports (e.g. MQTT via Amp) realise the synchronous `poll()` shape by suspending a Fiber internally; the kernel never sees the Fiber.

---

## 5. Encoding

### UADP

Part 14 §6.2 binary PubSub encoding. The codec supports:

- PublisherId types: Byte, UInt16, UInt32, UInt64, String
- GroupHeader with WriterGroupId, GroupVersion, NetworkMessageNumber, SequenceNumber
- PayloadHeader with one or more DataSetMessages (each with its own DataSetWriterId)
- Optional network-message Timestamp and PicoSeconds fields
- DataSetMessage flags: valid bit, FieldEncoding (Variant, RawData, DataValue), SequenceNumber, Timestamp, Status, ConfigurationVersion major and minor
- Only `DataKeyFrame` MessageType (the common case). `DeltaFrame`, `Event`, `KeepAlive` are rejected with a clear `PubSubDecodeException`
- Chunked NetworkMessages, PromotedFields, and non-DataSet NetworkMessageTypes are rejected

Security-wrapped payloads must be decrypted by `PubSubSecurityOptions::unwrap()` before the codec sees them; the codec throws if it encounters the Security flag on raw input.

### JSON

Reversible form from Part 14 §7.2.

```json
{
  "MessageId": "7c3c4e59-0a2e-4f8a-8b31-b0c1c0d0e1f2",
  "MessageType": "ua-data",
  "PublisherId": "100",
  "WriterGroupId": 1,
  "Messages": [
    {
      "DataSetWriterId": 7,
      "SequenceNumber": 42,
      "Status": 0,
      "Payload": {
        "temperature": { "Type": 11, "Body": 23.5 },
        "online":      { "Type": 1,  "Body": true }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Every field carries a `Type` (OPC UA `BuiltInType` int) so round-trip encode/decode does not need `DataSetMetaData`.

---

## 6. Security

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Security\PubSubSecurityMode;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Security\PubSubSecurityOptions;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Security\StaticGroupKeyProvider;

new PubSubSecurityOptions(
    mode: PubSubSecurityMode::SignAndEncrypt,
    keyProvider: new StaticGroupKeyProvider(
        signingKey: $hmacSha256Key,           // 32 bytes
        encryptingKey: $aes256CbcKey,         // 32 bytes
        keyNonce: $fixedGroupNonce,           // 16 bytes
    ),
);
```

`unwrap()` is called by the kernel on every received payload before decode. Modes:

- `None` — payload passes through untouched
- `Sign` — last 32 bytes are the HMAC-SHA256 signature over the preceding body; signature is verified and stripped
- `SignAndEncrypt` — signature is verified first (as above), then the remaining body is split into a 16-byte IV + AES-256-CBC ciphertext and decrypted

Rejected payloads raise `PubSubSecurityException` internally; the kernel catches this, drops the message, and dispatches a `SecurityValidationFailed` event. User callbacks never see tampered or undecryptable frames.

### SKS — live key rotation

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ClientBuilder;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Security\SksGroupKeyProvider;

$client = ClientBuilder::create()->connect('opc.tcp://sks.example.com:4840');

$provider = new SksGroupKeyProvider(
    client: $client,
    securityGroupId: 'line1-ops',
    // Optional overrides:
    // objectNodeId: 'i=14443',  // Server_PublishSubscribe
    // methodNodeId: 'i=15215',  // GetSecurityKeys
    // securityPolicyUri: SksGroupKeyProvider::POLICY_AES256_CTR,
    // requestedKeyCount: 1,
);

$provider->refresh();

$subscriber = SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->listenUdp(
        endpoint: 'opc.udp://239.0.0.1:4840',
        security: new PubSubSecurityOptions(PubSubSecurityMode::SignAndEncrypt, $provider),
        readers: [$reader],
    );
```

`refresh()` calls `GetSecurityKeys` (Part 14 §8.4.2) and splits the returned material according to the configured security policy (`PubSub-Aes256-CTR` = 32 + 32 + 4 bytes, `PubSub-Aes128-CTR` = 32 + 16 + 4 bytes). `lifetimeSecondsRemaining()` and `currentTokenId()` let user code decide when to call `refresh()` again.

---

## 7. Metadata

### From an array

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Types\DataSetMetaData;

$meta = DataSetMetaData::fromArray([
    'name' => 'LineOne',
    'majorVersion' => 1,
    'minorVersion' => 0,
    'description' => 'telemetry',
    'fields' => [
        ['name' => 'temperature', 'builtInType' => 11, 'valueRank' => -1],
        ['name' => 'pressure',    'builtInType' => 10],
    ],
]);
```

### From a JSON or XML file

```php
$meta = DataSetMetaData::fromJsonFile('/etc/opcua/line1.json');
$meta = DataSetMetaData::fromXmlFile('/etc/opcua/line1.xml');
```

The XML loader accepts both a compact `<DataSetMetaData>` / `<Field>` schema and the OPC UA canonical `<DataSetMetaDataType>` / `<FieldMetaData>` naming, so exports from NodeSet2 tooling work without preprocessing.

### From a running server

```php
$meta = DataSetMetaData::fetchFromServer($client, 'ns=2;s=PDS/Line1/Metadata');
```

Reads the Variable's `Value` attribute via the classic client. If the server value is a decoded DataSetMetaData (because the consumer registered an ExtensionObject codec for the type), the decoded instance is returned directly. Otherwise the raw binary ExtensionObject body is decoded via `DataSetMetaData::fromBinary()`.

### From binary

```php
$meta = DataSetMetaData::fromBinary($extensionObjectBody);
```

Walks the Part 14 §6.2.2 binary `DataSetMetaDataType` encoding. Non-empty nested schema sections (StructureDataTypes, EnumDataTypes, SimpleDataTypes) are rejected — v1 only handles the common flat case.

---

## 8. Events

8 PSR-14 event classes:

| Event | When |
|-------|------|
| `TransportOpened` | After a transport's `open()` succeeds |
| `TransportClosed` | After a transport's `close()` completes |
| `TransportError` | A transport raised an unexpected error during `open()` or `poll()` |
| `NetworkMessageReceived` | After a NetworkMessage has been decoded |
| `DataSetMessageReceived` | Once per DataSetMessage inside the NetworkMessage |
| `DataSetFieldReceived` | Once per decoded field |
| `MessageDecodeError` | A payload could not be decoded; the frame is dropped |
| `SecurityValidationFailed` | Signature verification or decryption failed |

All events carry the transport URI and the relevant decoded DTO (where applicable). Without an event dispatcher configured the kernel uses `NullPubSubEventDispatcher` (no-op).

---

## 9. Modules

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Module\PubSubModule;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Kernel\PubSubKernelInterface;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Types\DataSetMessage;

class AlarmForwarder extends PubSubModule
{
    public function boot(PubSubKernelInterface $kernel): void
    {
        parent::boot($kernel);
    }

    public function onDataSetMessage(
        DataSetMessage $message,
        int|string $publisherId,
        int $writerGroupId,
        string $transportUri,
    ): void {
        /* forward to alarms backend */
    }
}

$subscriber = SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->addModule(new AlarmForwarder())
    ->listenUdp(/* ... */);
```

`DataSetReaderModule` is always present (built automatically from the `readers` argument to `listenUdp` / `listenOn`). Additional modules are appended via `addModule()`.

---

## 10. Testing

Unit tests run without any external infrastructure. Integration tests (planned) require an `open62541` publisher in Docker — UA .NET Standard does not ship a UDP publisher out of the box.

```bash
./vendor/bin/pest                                          # everything
./vendor/bin/pest tests/Unit/                              # unit only
./vendor/bin/pest tests/Integration/ --group=integration   # integration only
php -d pcov.enabled=1 ./vendor/bin/pest --coverage         # with coverage
```

`tests/Unit/Kernel/FakeTransport.php` and `CollectingDispatcher.php` are helpers for driving the kernel in isolation:

```php
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Tests\Unit\Kernel\FakeTransport;
use PhpOpcua\Client\ExtTransportPubSub\Tests\Unit\Kernel\CollectingDispatcher;

$transport = new FakeTransport();
$transport->enqueue($rawBytes);

$dispatcher = new CollectingDispatcher();

$subscriber = SubscriberBuilder::create()
    ->setEventDispatcher($dispatcher)
    ->listenOn(transports: [$transport], readers: [$reader]);

$messages = $subscriber->poll(timeoutMs: 5);
assert($dispatcher->of(NetworkMessageReceived::class) !== []);
```

`SksGroupKeyProvider` and `DataSetMetaData::fetchFromServer` are exercised via the core's `MockClient` — no real server needed.

---

## 11. Requirements

- PHP >= 8.2 (tested on 8.2, 8.3, 8.4, 8.5)
- `ext-sockets`
- `ext-openssl` (transitive via the core)
- `php-opcua/opcua-client` ^4.4
- `psr/log` ^3.0, `psr/event-dispatcher` ^1.0
